title: diasperus stocksii
intro_study: According to "BMC complementary and alternative medicine", Diasperus stocksii (also known as a component of Diasulin) has antihyperlipidemic and antiperoxidative effects, in addition to its antidiabetic properties.
side_effects: Diasperus stocksii can have side effects if used improperly, such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to its potential interaction with other medications. In some cases, excessive consumption may lead to liver damage and increased blood pressure. Additionally, individuals with pre-existing kidney conditions may experience worsening symptoms, including kidney failure, if they ingest the plant without proper guidance. Furthermore, improper preparation of Diasperus stocksii can also cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching.
